metadata.title:
LEGALITY


Of CLAUSE


ATTACKED

LAWYERS SAY TERMINAL RATE
PROVISION WILL NOT


STAND.

1WOWR|TTEN OPINIONS

pages.raw_text:
In written opinions submitted to.
day by request to A. W.. Doland,
chairman of the interstate COM
merce commission Of the chamber
Of commerce, Cyrus Happy and
James M. Geraahty, attorneys, hold
that the city cannot enforce a ter
ninai rate clause in the franchises
Of tho Milwaukee and the North
Coast and that IL the same were
granted it would be null and void.


These opinions will be read to-
night at the meeting of the chamber
of commerce on the franchise mat
ter and published in the newspapers
in advertisement form tomorrow.


1t is held that the city, in trying
to enforce terminal rates by fran
chise contract, is exceeding the au-
thority reposing in it as munici.
pal corporation. Such a contract is
held to be special contract and
contrary to the interstate commerce
law. Decisions are cited to show
that franchise contracts of this
character are not upheld by the
courts. and that violations of the
same have been held as not invali-
dating the franchise.


H. M. Stephens. attorney in the
Spokane rate case, and Frank
Graves, attorney for the Hill inter
ests, believe that the terminal rate
provision will stand.
